paper_title: TASK-BASED LANGUAGE TEACHING AND ITS IMPACT ON VIETNAMESE STUDENTS' USE OF SELF-REGULATED LEARNING STRATEGIES IN A WRITING CLASSROOM

entities.description: The author of the research paper.

entities.id: School of Foreign Languages, Can Tho University
entities.description: The academic institution where the author is affiliated.
The primary research field of the paper focusing on language teaching.
An analytical approach to language pedagogy used to instruct students in this study.
The process by which students are metacognitively, motivationally, and behaviorally active in their own learning.
Specific strategies used by students to regulate environmental, behavioral, and personal processes in writing.
A 30-statement instrument based on Zimmerman and Risemberg’s model using a 7-point Likert-scale.
Qualitative research instrument conducted in small groups to share ideas and experiences.
Observational method used to provide supportive evidence for students' self-regulatory strategies.
Statistical analysis used to measure improvements in scores over time.

entities.id: Pretest, immediate posttest, and delayed posttest data
entities.description: The longitudinal numerical data collected from sixty-nine student participants.
Reliability score of the questionnaire used in the study.
Mean score of self-regulatory writing strategies before the course.
Mean score of self-regulatory writing strategies ten weeks after the course.
Initial mean score for personal process self-regulation.
Mean score for personal process self-regulation right after the writing course.
Mean score for personal process self-regulation ten weeks after the writing course.
High effect size calculated between pretest and delayed posttest for overall strategies.
